prompt: Here's my full profile: Dream project — a personal finance tracker that pulls from my bank API. Background — Python, 3 years, mostly data stuff. Frustration — I spend way too long writing docstrings and comments. Claude Code experience — used it a couple times, feels powerful but I don't know what I'm doing. Excitement — I want to stop doing the repetitive stuff so I can focus on the actual hard problems.
assertions: Synthesis is 2-3 sentences and names specific details from their answers (finance tracker, docstrings, Python)
Synthesis reads as a genuine reflection — not a bullet-point recap of what the user said
Response frames the first task around the finance tracker goal or docstring frustration
Response builds anticipation — the first task feels like the start of something real
Response does NOT list all 8 lessons or give a course syllabus
